title: Rule-Debt State Machine Features
summary: FIA rules create hard discontinuities that the current feature sets only approximate.
rationale: FIA rules create hard discontinuities that the current feature sets only approximate. Dry races require two dry specs unless wet tyres are used, and Monaco has a special mandatory two-stop rule from 2025, so pit urgency should jump when a driver still owes a compound or stop and laps remaining shrink.
implementation_hint: Build per driver-race chronological state: dry compounds already used, wet-usage flag, pit-count-to-date, remaining mandatory-stop debt, remaining mandatory-compound debt, and a Monaco-2025 special-case remaining-stop counter. Interact these with laps remaining, stint, and compound.
expected_effect: Should improve late-stint precision and reduce validation noise from rows where pit probability is rule-forced rather than degradation-driven.
risk: If race metadata is incomplete or Monaco rows are sparse, the special-case features may overfit and need shrinkage or simple binary flags only.
